attack_surface.data_description: User interaction with what they believe is the CEX's legitimate website or application.

security_gap.gap_description: Users may be tricked into entering credentials or connecting wallets to a fake CEX interface, leading to loss of credentials or assets.

actor_risk.description: An external attacker creates a convincing fake CEX interface to steal user assets or credentials.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Phishing is a common and effective attack vector targeting users of centralized services like CEXs.

assumptions: Users access CEX services through web interfaces or applications.
Attackers can create convincing replicas of CEX interfaces.
Users can be tricked by social engineering or misleading links into interacting with the fake interface.

mitigation.strategy: User education on phishing prevention (e.g., checking URLs, using bookmarks). CEXs implementing anti-phishing measures (e.g., official domain lists, 2FA, anti-phishing codes in emails).

description: An attacker could create a spoofed (fake) CEX website or application to trick users into revealing their credentials or depositing $BERA to attacker-controlled addresses, leading to fund loss. The CEX (ID 9) is the entity being spoofed.
